preamble: The shift from [b] to [dz] is a complex transformation that typically involves a palatalization stage where a labial consonant develops into a dental or alveolar affricate.

examples.note: Stages where labials palatalize to affricates before certain vowels or glides.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
